## Trump Suggests Reducing China Tariffs
President Trump said Friday morning he was open to slashing [tariffs on China's goods to 80%](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkL-O2zAMh5_G3mhIshM5g4Yu3q5rR4NnURELWTIkJkHevvC16AscuPEfft_362P9QE6c7z9xJyehrJleLZEI1XWLnFGwcghtL00qJcbPRI_VKHMBdQGtVo-c3uvUexeIDI09OW2n62z1PM19dMbqkWbSytsx-G3GT0V2mq1Bv3lUY8_u_KYuWp012UEpj5v2N2X0eLtetm5S5FlKHX4XzhIppHd5Uh22svfJRZGjdeOPziydWQJnzBsNb4ylnBudWU6kziyJn9SZRepjP-AfFpxNeBwehRr8HYVUUBq0hC1yvsOXhv8HUmBW8GKJsGN-gydMDTiDRIJYjoMq6Js2SturHqLsqa_OHy9OidvVdpO678jpK7x8y_jTmT8BAAD__9ggl7o), down from the 145% rate set earlier, as the two nations prepare to start trade talks this weekend. The news comes as the [first freight ships carrying Chinese cargo](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkLHOpCAUhZ8GOwwgoBYU29jNtlsaBi56NwgGmDHz9hsn-wZ_bnKbk3w53_nzWB8WI6bttz3AtJDXBFeN0BqU1e2YbLMFQ6hHrq1ARPuM8FoFE4oyRTlbvcX4WWXnTQAQMHRg-Cj1NPJJTt1utAizEnrmSk3eMae15uNT-kFBgFHrDs1NY4qz--TYM-at435mgg-zVo5IBh5bLv3fjKntEOInv6H0Lh9dNHtrZyXDLyIWIpbrunqXnu4OiVhuNBEL-76ZiCVgqY3eZlCBbjn7SrlUJxQHqdEzvir9r0xtKfjGtNFXpWcurfZ7O2JXjD8vjBGrHolk22Exfru0Hw34NuJfAAAA___9xIP4) began arriving at ports in Los Angeles and Long Beach, loaded with goods for Amazon, Home Depot, Ikea, Procter & Gamble, and others. Trump said in a Truth Social post Friday that the ultimate tariff level was up to U.S. Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent, who's scheduled to meet with Chinese Vice Premier He Lifeng in Switzerland today to open negotiations.
## Group Reveals Secret Iranian Nuke Facility
New satellite images have revealed an alleged secret [Iranian nuclear weapons facility](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkDGP4yAUhH-N3WEBgdguKK5Jl2uvtF7gYb_TM0RA4s2_X3n_wmq6GWk08_27L3cgprT-hR1di3lJeFTG1rAsfqMEDQrFWPdcW0EmeDC-Fi21FdIKJZcAxJ_F9MFFRI2XHp0azXUa1WSmfnNzCMp7aSJEYx5Wj0rb2Y_SRj1pKUNP7myTVslTZhykDOBVmKVWl_lqfWckBmq5DP8zpbZh5E9-Yxl83nt2W2vP2l3-dPrW6dtxHEPMX-eLMz-dXDh0-lahITM1FLTDilUUfCOwAGZcMYiKvmATVCARJJFenhGKOBCeOVURwRNT-_TFhedBzFSvY2fkugPxz5T2K35vp78DAAD__xO1h2k) that’s been in operation for over a decade.

The newly identified site, code-named the “Rainbow Site,” is far from the nation’s already known nuclear facilities. The Iranian government, which denies the claims, says the site is a chemical production factory. The allegations, [including aerial photos](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkL2O5CAQhJ_GzrAwxn8BwSXO5tILrR5oxn3CMGqYQfP2K6_2DVYddCX1qar-3fYbUKD4-AsnmuLTHrHmgKUg7_agCAWYvM9nyoUxENwDvnYl1SjkKHq5O6Dw2XXrjEdUOLRo-llPy9wvemkPA6uaZ4WoFn8HN-vJaTWsg-xX6fWqXUvmosmxl9fpuZPSge3dKlU_rNNoGy3RUUnc_U8Uy4E-fNIbubPpbIM5SnnmZvjTqK1RW621i5YFMcQu8aNRG8ZGbVet671sQOBLWSbB-EYIWWS0jEUwULynKgpTodcpPFgKVD7CJxY_VnFSzhRQVOADwV3Ulo17VgqB8jQ3Wj5OoPAdr_xq07dRXwEAAP__F22Nfg), were made public by the National Council of Resistance of Iran, a group the Iranian government labels as a terrorist organization. The group says the site is “heavily fortified” with radar and air defense systems.

The U.S. is currently in negotiations with Iran over its nuclear program. Earlier this week, Vice President JD Vance said the talks remain on track.

body:

| | | [](http://trk.tedcruz._-a03GnesCsOPcF5fA)

| | Fellow Conservative, Leftists love to proclaim they're driven by altruism and principle. That they're passionate about building a better world and fighting the good fight against forces of evil.

Some of them really believe it. Some just want others to believe it.

The fact is across the board a whole lot of them are **DERANGED LUNATICS** and the loudest voices in their movement and in Congress are **SHAMELESS HYPOCRITES**...

Just look at how their head over heels love of Tesla - one of the largest manufacturers of electric vehicles on the planet - has quickly turned to unhinged hatred. They're engaged in a coordinated campaign of vandalism of personal property and they've SHOT UP and FIREBOMBED a number of Tesla dealerships.

**Their Trump Derangement Syndrome is OFF THE CHARTS!**

I'm calling out these woke radicals every chance I get and I will never back down.** ** [_**RUSH SUPPORT TO FUEL OUR FIGHT AT TEAM CRUZ >>>**_](http://trk.tedcruz._PrMKw)
---
[ **DONATE TO HELP** ](http://trk.tedcruz._eAgBOqNs5ZRhnUhIw)
---

**They work up crocodile tears for the cameras about saving the planet, while they fly around on private jets and now want to DESTROY Tesla over Elon Musk 's role in rooting out government corruption and waste.**

We've kicked the radical left out of power in Washington and now we need to keep them out of power in the 2026 midterms and beyond. Their ultimate goal is control over ALL of us - _we cannot let them trample our freedom and destroy our economy and way of life._

Thank you for any critical support you can send. It makes all the difference in our efforts.

For Liberty,

Ted Cruz

name: Elizabeth Nader
subject: The NJ Transit mess is getting worse
body:
Peter, You guys. The cruelty and dishonesty of the Murphy administration and his cronies in Trenton knows no bounds. NJ Transit is about to raise fares while we continue to experience chronic delays. How on earth ar
| | [ ]()
---
Peter, You guys. The cruelty and dishonesty of the Murphy administration and his cronies in Trenton knows no bounds. NJ Transit is about to raise fares _while we continue to experience chronic delays._ How on earth are we supposed to start paying more for a system that is working SO poorly? But even more disturbing is this new revelation. New Jersey Transit engineers have been underpaid for years, and there is a significant gap between what they earn working for NJ Transit and what their fellow locomotive engineers earn at other railroads. To offset their substandard wages, NJ Transit engineers have to work a lot of overtime. The Brotherhood of Locomotive Engineers (BLET) has tried to negotiate a modest wage increase to ensure engineers are paid commensurately with their peers at other railroads. But NJ Transit is refusing – despite the fact that they are actively losing engineers to other railroads (cue more delays) because of the poor wages. If no deal is reached, engineers will strike and the trains will stop moving in less than a week. But who do _Governor Murphy’s Leftist Lunatic allies_ want to blame for the coming fare hikes at NJ Transit – even though they have refused to offer BLET a fair wage deal – the engineers! NJ Transit has mismanaged their already bloated funds. But sure, blame the engineers who just want to make a fair wage. Murphy’s NJ Transit is broken and ultimately, his administration’s refusal to negotiate fairly will hurt _us_ the commuters. Had enough of the madness? Us too. VOTE it out – starting in the June 10th primary.
---

Crapo Backs Bipartisan Bill to Expand Housing Access for Disabled Veterans
May 7, 2025, Daily Fly

“WASHINGTON, D.C. – U.S. Senator Mike Crapo (R-Idaho) has joined a bipartisan group of lawmakers to introduce the Housing Unhoused Disabled Veterans Act (HUDVA), aimed at ensuring that disabled veterans experiencing homelessness are not denied housing support due to their disability compensation. Senators Alex Padilla (D-California), Dave McCormick (R-Pennsylvania), Ruben Gallego (D-Arizona), and Katie Britt (R-Alabama) joined Crapo in sponsoring the legislation. The bill seeks to permanently exclude Veterans Affairs (VA) disability benefits from being counted as income when determining eligibility for the U.S. Department of Housing and Urban Development-Veterans Affairs Supportive Housing (HUD-VASH) program. The HUD-VASH program provides rental assistance and support
services to veterans facing homelessness. Historically, some veterans with severe disabilities were disqualified from receiving HUD-VASH assistance because their disability payments pushed their income above eligibility thresholds. “Veterans who placed their lives on the line and were disabled in active duty combat should not have to worry about whether or not they will have a home after they return from their service,” Crapo stated in the release. While HUD updated its policy last year to exclude VA disability benefits from income calculations for HUD-VASH, the HUDVA legislation would codify this change, making it a permanent part of the program’s eligibility rules. The bill is also supported by Senators Richard Blumenthal (D-Connecticut), Bill Cassidy (R-Louisiana),
and Mazie Hirono (D-Hawaii). Companion legislation is being introduced in the U.S. House by Representatives Brad Sherman (D-California) and Monica De La Cruz (R-Texas). Senator Crapo is also a co-lead sponsor of the Major Richard Star Act, which seeks to provide full VA disability and Department of Defense retirement benefits to combat-injured veteran retirees.”

Senate bill aims to raise FHA multifamily loan limits
Yahoo Finance

“Two U.S. Senators introduced a bill on April 30 that, if passed, would raise the Federal Housing Administration’s limits on multifamily mortgage principals for the first time in 22 years. SB 1527, also known as the Housing Affordability Act, aims to increase the supply of affordable housing by providing more funds to its developers, according to a news release from its co-authors — Sens. Ruben Gallego, D-Ariz., and Dave McCormick, R-Penn. The FHA’s multifamily loan limits, intended to prevent HUD funds from going to high-end projects, have not been raised since 2003. As a result, the dollar volume of HUD-issued multifamily firm commitments has dropped, and almost all communities are treated as “high-cost areas” due to the costs of their construction,
according to Gallego and McCormick. “To tackle the affordable housing crisis, we need to make building homes easier and cheaper. But outdated regulations are holding us back,” Gallego said in the news release. “By updating a nearly two-decade-old loan limit, the Housing Affordability Act will expand access to affordable loans for building multifamily housing and ultimately bring down housing costs.” The proposed limits, calculated by units, are up to four times the previous limit. For instance, if passed, the bill would raise per-unit mortgage loan limits for rental housing in buildings without elevators from $38,025 to $167,310 for a studio apartment, $42,120 to $185,328 for a one-bedroom unit and $50,310 to $221,364 for a two-bedroom unit, among other changes.
The act would also update the inflationary adjustment index from the Consumer Price Index to the Price Deflator Index of Multifamily Residential Units Under Construction, published by the U.S. Census Bureau. The bill has been referred to the Senate Committee on Banking, Housing, and Urban Affairs, according to congressional records. “By making a long-overdue increase to the FHA’s multifamily loan limits, we can incentivize the construction of more apartment buildings and, ultimately, increase access to housing for American families,” McCormick said in the news release. The bill is endorsed by over a dozen housing associations, including the National Multifamily Housing Council, the National Apartment Association and the Mortgage Bankers Association. “The Housing
Affordability Act would make a long-overdue update to FHA’s multifamily insurance programs to capture the true cost of apartment construction and immediately leverage private capital for new affordable homes,” said Bill Killmer, senior vice president of legislative and political affairs at the MBA, in the release.”

Senate Republicans push to defund global orgs that grant Palestine Liberation Organization special privileges
NY Post

“WASHINGTON — A new bill by Senate Republicans would cut funding to the United Nations — and other international organizations — that offer the Palestine Liberation Organization (PLO) special privileges, according to text of the bill exclusively shared with The Post. The No Official Palestine Entry Act of 2025 would tighten a US law that bars subsidizing organizations that grant the representative body of Palestinian people the same standing as member UN states. It would extend it to any international organization that gives the PLO “any status, rights or privileges beyond observer status.” “Once created to be a bastion of peace and security in the world, the United Nations is now a seat of antisemitism and in desperate need of reform,”
Senate Foreign Relations Committee Chairman James Risch (R-Idaho) said. “Israel is one of America’s greatest allies and we cannot tolerate or fund any anti-Israel bias or favoritism for the Palestinian [sic] Liberation Organization at the UN,” he added. The bill, introduced Tuesday morning by the SFRC, comes a week after Risch introduced the Stand With Israel Act, which would slash funding to UN agencies that downgrade or restrict the participation of the Jewish State. “These bills will ensure that America has Israel’s back when it matters most,” Risch said. It also comes a day after Israel announced it would dramatically ramp up its military offensive against Hamas by seizing the whole of Gaza, which the terrorist group governed. The bill is sponsored
by Risch, Tom Cotton (R-Ark.), Mike Lee (R-Utah), James Lankford (R-Okla.), Lindsey Graham and Tim Scott (R-S.C.), Ted Cruz and John Cornyn (R-Texas), Mike Crapo (R-Idaho), Dave McCormick (R-Pa.)...”

Breitbart News

“The Biden State Department violated federal law to open the OPA and it only served to sabotage Israel at every turn in the wake of Hamas’s terror attacks,” Sen. Dave McCormick (R-PA) wrote in a post on X. “This is the correct move by President Trump, Secretary Rubio, and Ambassador Huckabee to support our great ally and reverse the previous administration’s undermining of Israel.” During President Donald Trump’s first term he delivered a video address during the opening of the official United States Embassy in Jerusalem on May 14, 2018, Breitbart News reported. “Today we follow through on this recognition and open our embassy in the historic and sacred land of Jerusalem and we’re opening it many, many years ahead of schedule,”

Dave Portnoy receives praise for handling of antisemitic incident at Barstool bar Fox News

“Ben Stiller, Sen. Dave McCormick and others praised Barstool Sports founder Dave Portnoy for how he dealt with the culprits who initiated the "f--- the Jews" sign at one of the company’s bars. Portnoy said in a video on Sunday that the two men who initiated the antisemitic signage at the Philadelphia establishment were going to be sent to Auschwitz in Poland to learn about the Holocaust. More than 1.1 million Jews were killed in Nazi concentration camps at the site. "Good on @stoolpresidente for not only immediately working to address the horrific display of hate displayed in Philadelphia, the City of Brotherly Love, last night but also using it as an opportunity to educate on the tragic history of anti-Jewish hatred and violence," McCormick wrote. "We can’t look the
other way at antisemitism. It needs to be identified, called out, and crushed whenever it rears its ugly head." Stiller "agreed" with Portnoy on going scorched-earth on the culprits. However, Portnoy eventually cooled off and decided to educate the men on the horrors of the Holocaust instead. "Everyone needs to call out antisemitism when they see it. Thank you @stoolpresidente for doing the real work to ensure change," X CEO Linda Yaccarino wrote…”

The Mercury’s Sound Off for Monday, May 5
Pottstown Daily News

“Jay Allen’s post on April 25th that Senator Dave McCormick (R-PA) has not championed any legislation is blatantly false. In just his first few months in office, Sen. McCormick introduced a new amendment to the U.S. Constitution to institute term limits for members of Congress, and he sponsored a bill, along with John Fetterman (D-PA), to combat illicit synthetic narcotics. –Robert Minninger.”

U.S. ag secretary Rollins visits Pa.
The Lewistown Sentinel

“WASHINGTON — U.S. Secretary of Agriculture Brooke Rollins visited Central Pennsylvania in April where she toured Talview Dairy, participated in a farmer roundtable at Martin’s family farm, and visited the Bank of Bird-in-Hand mobile bank to discuss agricultural lending with community leaders. Secretary Rollins was joined by Senator Dave McCormick, House Agriculture Committee Chairman GT Thompson, and Representatives Dan Meuser, Rob Bresnahan and Lloyd Smucker. Secretary Rollins also wrote a joint opinion piece wrapping up the visit with Senator McCormick. “President Trump and I are fighting to put farmers first. I heard firsthand about the issues farmers are facing in Central Pennsylvania. I look forward to continuing our work in Washington to ensure that the
farmers and ranchers who make our country great can continue to thrive for generations to come,” said Secretary Rollins. “Agriculture is the largest industry in Pennsylvania and farming is the backbone of our economy. I am grateful for Secretary Rollins for coming to our Commonwealth to see Pennsylvania ag in action. From dairy operations, world class chicken facilities, rural banking and lenders for farmers, and everything in between the breadth and scope of our farming community is vast and the future is bright with Secretary Rollins at the helm. She and I are working together with our entire delegation to promote Pennsylvania agriculture domestically and around the world through good policies and opening markets,” said Senator McCormick…”

I'm Dave McCormick. I'm a West Point grad, combat veteran who served in the 82nd Airborne, a 7th-generation Pennsylvanian, and last November I defeated an entrenched career politician in the #1 swing state of the 2024 election.

I’ve seen firsthand what great leadership looks like. I’ve seen that even on our worst days, you have to put the needs of others first.

That’s why I made a promise to come to the Senate and work with fellow political outsiders like President Trump to shake up Washington, and I’m working to do that every single day.

Whether it’s securing the border, reducing crime in our cities, ending the fentanyl epidemic, or bringing down the cost of living–I’m committed to delivering concrete solutions to the biggest issues we face.

But in order to get the job done, it’s going to take all hands on deck. I need every freedom-loving American to help me shake up Washington. I promise I won't let you down.

Senator Dave McCormick

The bill is endorsed by over a dozen housing associations, including the National Multifamily Housing Council, the National Apartment Association and the Mortgage Bankers Association. “The Housing Affordability Act would make a long-overdue update to FHA’s multifamily insurance programs to capture the true cost of apartment construction and immediately leverage private capital for new affordable homes,” said Bill Killmer, senior vice president of legislative and political affairs at the MBA, in the release.” Senate Republicans push to defund global orgs that grant Palestine Liberation Organization special privileges NY Post “WASHINGTON — A new bill by Senate Republicans would cut funding to the United Nations — and other international organizations — that offer the Palestine Liberation Organization (PLO) special privileges, according to text of the bill exclusively shared with The Post. The No Official Palestine Entry Act of 2025 would tighten a US law that bars subsidizing organizations that grant the representative body of Palestinian people the same standing as member UN states. It would extend it to any international organization that gives the PLO “any status, rights or privileges beyond observer status.” “Once created to be a bastion of peace and security in the world, the United Nations is now a seat of antisemitism and in desperate need of reform,” Senate Foreign Relations Committee Chairman James Risch (R-Idaho) said. “Israel is one of America’s greatest allies and we cannot tolerate or fund any anti-Israel bias or favoritism for the Palestinian [sic] Liberation Organization at the UN,” he added. The bill, introduced Tuesday morning by the SFRC, comes a week after Risch introduced the Stand With Israel Act, which would slash funding to UN agencies that downgrade or restrict the participation of the Jewish State. “These bills will ensure that America has Israel’s back when it matters most,” Risch said. It also comes a day after Israel announced it would dramatically ramp up its military offensive against Hamas by seizing the whole of Gaza, which the terrorist group governed. The bill is sponsored by Risch, Tom Cotton (R-Ark.), Mike Lee (R-Utah), James Lankford (R-Okla.), Lindsey Graham and Tim Scott (R-S.C.), Ted Cruz and John Cornyn (R-Texas), Mike Crapo (R-Idaho), Dave McCormick (R-Pa.)...” Explained: The Antisemitic Incident at Dave Portnoy’s Barstool Sports Philly Bar American Jewish Committee “Republican Pennsylvania Sen. Dave McCormick commended him for addressing the “horrific display of hate” and using it as an opportunity to educate about anti-Jewish violence, saying, “Antisemitism needs to be identified, called out, and crushed.” Marco Rubio Orders State Department to Close Office of Palestinian Affairs Breitbart News “The Biden State Department violated federal law to open the OPA and it only served to sabotage Israel at every turn in the wake of Hamas’s terror attacks,” Sen. Dave McCormick (R-PA) wrote in a post on X. “This is the correct move by President Trump, Secretary Rubio, and Ambassador Huckabee to support our great ally and reverse the previous administration’s undermining of Israel.” During President Donald Trump’s first term he delivered a video address during the opening of the official United States Embassy in Jerusalem on May 14, 2018, Breitbart News reported. “Today we follow through on this recognition and open our embassy in the historic and sacred land of Jerusalem and we’re opening it many, many years ahead of schedule,” Trump said at the time.” Dave Portnoy receives praise for handling of antisemitic incident at Barstool bar Fox News “Ben Stiller, Sen. Dave McCormick and others praised Barstool Sports founder Dave Portnoy for how he dealt with the culprits who initiated the "f--- the Jews" sign at one of the company’s bars. Portnoy said in a video on Sunday that the two men who initiated the antisemitic signage at the Philadelphia establishment were going to be sent to Auschwitz in Poland to learn about the Holocaust. More than 1.1 million Jews were killed in Nazi concentration camps at the site. "Good on @stoolpresidente for not only immediately working to address the horrific display of hate displayed in Philadelphia, the City of Brotherly Love, last night but also using it as an opportunity to educate on the tragic history of anti-Jewish hatred and violence," McCormick wrote. "We can’t look the other way at antisemitism. It needs to be identified, called out, and crushed whenever it rears its ugly head." Stiller "agreed" with Portnoy on going scorched-earth on the culprits. However, Portnoy eventually cooled off and decided to educate the men on the horrors of the Holocaust instead. "Everyone needs to call out antisemitism when they see it. Thank you @stoolpresidente for doing the real work to ensure change," X CEO Linda Yaccarino wrote…” Dave Portnoy Enraged After Antisemitic Incident at His Bar Breitbart News “Actor Ben Stiller, Pennsylvania’s Republican Sen. Dave McCormick, and others applauded Portnoy for the thoughtful reaction to the hateful incident and praised him for taking the high road, Fox News reported. “Good on @stoolpresidente for not only immediately working to address the horrific display of hate displayed in Philadelphia, the City of Brotherly Love, last night but also using it as an opportunity to educate on the tragic history of anti-Jewish hatred and violence,” Sen. McCormick wrote on social media. “We can’t look the other way at antisemitism. It needs to be identified, called out, and crushed whenever it rears its ugly head,” he added…” The Mercury’s Sound Off for Monday, May 5 Pottstown Daily News “Jay Allen’s post on April 25th that Senator Dave McCormick (R-PA) has not championed any legislation is blatantly false. In just his first few months in office, Sen. McCormick introduced a new amendment to the U.S. Constitution to institute term limits for members of Congress, and he sponsored a bill, along with John Fetterman (D-PA), to combat illicit synthetic narcotics. –Robert Minninger.” U.S. ag secretary Rollins visits Pa. The Lewistown Sentinel “WASHINGTON — U.S. Secretary of Agriculture Brooke Rollins visited Central Pennsylvania in April where she toured Talview Dairy, participated in a farmer roundtable at Martin’s family farm, and visited the Bank of Bird-in-Hand mobile bank to discuss agricultural lending with community leaders. Secretary Rollins was joined by Senator Dave McCormick, House Agriculture Committee Chairman GT Thompson, and Representatives Dan Meuser, Rob Bresnahan and Lloyd Smucker. Secretary Rollins also wrote a joint opinion piece wrapping up the visit with Senator McCormick. “President Trump and I are fighting to put farmers first. I heard firsthand about the issues farmers are facing in Central Pennsylvania. I look forward to continuing our work in Washington to ensure that the farmers and ranchers who make our country great can continue to thrive for generations to come,” said Secretary Rollins. “Agriculture is the largest industry in Pennsylvania and farming is the backbone of our economy. I am grateful for Secretary Rollins for coming to our Commonwealth to see Pennsylvania ag in action. From dairy operations, world class chicken facilities, rural banking and lenders for farmers, and everything in between the breadth and scope of our farming community is vast and the future is bright with Secretary Rollins at the helm. She and I are working together with our entire delegation to promote Pennsylvania agriculture domestically and around the world through good policies and opening markets,” said Senator McCormick…” This Monday’s Question of the Week asked Do you believe the U.S. should prioritize energy independence? Here are the results: → YES: 97% → NO: 1% → UNSURE: 2% Here are some of your responses. - Susan W.: “Thanks for all your hard work you do for us!!” - Richard F: “America First and Drill Baby Drill!” - Shari S.: “Energy independence is much more than an economic issue. It is a matter of national security.” - John P.: “Get the pipeline open & oil flowing!”Keep an eye out for next week’s Question of the Week in your inbox every Monday to let Dave know what you think. Thanks, Team McCormick DONATE NOW I'm Dave McCormick. I'm a West Point grad, combat veteran who served in the 82nd Airborne, a 7th-generation Pennsylvanian, and last November I defeated an entrenched career politician in the #1 swing state of the 2024 election. I’ve seen firsthand what great leadership looks like. I’ve seen that even on our worst days, you have to put the needs of others first. That’s why I made a promise to come to the Senate and work with fellow political outsiders like President Trump to shake up Washington, and I’m working to do that every single day. Whether it’s securing the border, reducing crime in our cities, ending the fentanyl epidemic, or bringing down the cost of living–I’m committed to delivering concrete solutions to the biggest issues we face. But in order to get the job done, it’s going to take all hands on deck. I need every freedom-loving American to help me shake up Washington. I promise I won't let you down. Senator Dave McCormick DONATE Paid for by Friends of Dave McCormick.

# Saturday, May 10, 2025
---|---
**Good Morning!** On this day in 1893, the [Texas Equal Rights Association](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJyk0DuSqzAQheHVQAbVEu9AwU3IfNMJqRZqQ9cIySO1jb37KXsLs4HvnPq_LssF2XPY_uNBRp7XuAQ6sycRSovH4DJ6shjWeA-SXsc984rnokF3FXSVgsUh-9fSls5YcJMqyaih7cehHSdV7mYi20_XtmvW0XbaQYuEGrVrwPY9NkPJ5o1Bp0ApUHqsLTpHKwyqAdU7GIoWyLHEVMtOQk_MV_-KD0r1Go_Sm13klovmX6HnQs_nedaSd4zBc6A6pq3Q847B2Ri_Cz1TkMSUCz1_qIp-7uirxNsuucKc48ooHEOZjLud7D3n_n1hO5D9Z1H-1ulh9G8AAAD__88Df2k) was formed in Dallas to advocate for women’s suffrage, becoming the first such organization in the state. Speaking of women, two of them came up big in the [ACM Awards](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkL2uozAUhJ8GOpB_wHYKF9vQZdst0cHnJLFkMLJNHN5-Re4j3HY-afTN_LvPd_DBb8-_sJItn0ecN6o5UCmU5gAbZgi0wObisZV0rkf2DuosmBg7NnaczQg-nPPQol0Y3nhLlutBGT2YG29fljljlDLI1bhoaZQkkFLTgzstDKFsvb3K2MgZ54wL0y-ASI5pLhlXyHQzMEJfYurLiwp9ID_CGd-UehfXNthXKXtu5J9GTI2Yaq19iQjnRRsx7XF3RyhHokZMUCFhbsQU_Ju6JcTnFbq1-wHdd9aXHTtCodwlt4Fgo7ipNlncqw_BZ3U5PVfw4atQfnfc24r_AQAA___C4IJ2) Thursday night in Frisco, which is one of several music items in today's edition. Don't forget to kick off your weekend with our weekend podcast host, Amy Hess! [Click here](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkLGOszAQhJ8GOtAuMRgXLv4mXf7qpJRoYTfBkoEIlji8_Ym7R7h2RvON9N1v3Y1CDPPzP03i9fNYulnSFkVV1i7SzBtF6Wkeln3W9Zj2LQyUugqquoC6QOiYQjw6k7PvgR3m4tGaprWmdZiPnsg12LcOwBh20tp6eDC1KFYcW3R58CcMagREwKote2KWASxeABsGmxkQDrqspY6i8qHtEY_lLWs5LFMe_aj62rLLv6y6ZtU1pVQey657L2d_hgbupMNYfI1SXH-n-er5lUKMYWvOg-dEIf7w9G8W3r76DgAA__8YYnGe). Later today, we will email you on behalf of our partner [Neurozoom,](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykzzuO6yAUgOHVQJfoHGyMU1Dcxl1uOyXicRwjYYiAvGb1I88Wpv_1Sf_X1VxtTDHf_tuddH-vxWR6tUS9UzXJ5tBsImezL4_c62d_tOjtywgQ8gTyhGCCjeljRh60g3BBThrVOM1qnC_INw1o51l6JcUIk1tJglNOhQEvuKphcDzqAwOJgAgo5rOzIZAHhQPgFECxESjEXuq5b9TpbduaPuVJ9ezLzpPeer83NvxjYmFiuVHP9Kjlu5T9CJhYnnjyW4memFh41eH-iinFNh3ybbcx_UL9b_tPLX4CAAD__6hBbqo) with information to help deal with memory loss issues. We hope you find it worth exploring. And today, we have a new sponsor. [Cemetery Preservation Supply](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJyk0DuOrDAQheHVQGZUNu_AwU3I-qYTosIuwFJht2w3NLsf9cwSJj86n_R_PeYHOnZ--48H6fxew-zpSkw5U5wZvU3ItKA34eVzvI9XcgavWYFqBbRCwmzR8T03pdUL2FGWpGXfdEPfDKMsd21gsNBKbOsBeqRuXdVIIJdlHGrbIJVOf86glSAlSDVUC1pLBnpZg-ws9EUDZF0Osco7ZXpjWvkOJ8XKhKNkvef8TEX9r1BToabruqot4kkpB0-GCf3vslDTQiYcJPJOYqcYRFjFHV5RrHg4vpNg2tDchZrKqO3zcswudR9_O9DxD5f_FunU6jsAAP__O4F97Q) is a Texas-based business that can provide everything needed to maintain and restore your loved one’s gravestone.

## House Passes Land Sale Ban on China, Others
The Texas House passed a bill Friday that would [prohibit the sale](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkLGutCAQhZ9GOwygrm5B8Td2-7e3NAOMOgmCAVzXt79hX-F2U8w5-b7z85pfQI78-h92VPmzhNnjlRzmjHF24G0Chxq8CafP8d7PRAauWXLZM94zwWcL5O65q63S3D5FjUoM3WMcuvEp6k0BSgPWam7NoNsWRjRilHZpR-x7kGNNqpTxXnAhuJBjo8FaNHwQLRcPy4eq42gph9jkDTN-IC3uDm-MjQl77dSW85Gq9l8lp0pO13U1S_h0RaM8VHIqZyWnb5Rt4UzI4DhieGNimpxjjnbK5FdWhFkxTiyFHdkSItLqmT5vjKmOyh4XOUfpUajWHch9IfLfpnsr-RsAAP__c9qHUg) of Texas “real property” to entities from China, Russia, Iran, and North Korea, all of which are considered national security threats by the federal government. The bill, known as Senate Bill 17, would also give authority to Gov. Greg Abbott to [add other nations](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkL2unDAQRp8GOq_8B4aCIg3dTZsSjfHAjjJrr2yz3H37iI2UF0g3mk860jm_vpYvIKa4_4QHTvV7S0vEszDWinlhiKEAo4e4piPW_H4chVY4Fy11J2QnlFwCEL8X24bJyzCqFiflbD84O4yqvU-9V0aPCpXELjhvDeCwrtKMxmy9H6Cl6YLJTkmlpNLDzUMIuEqnjFR9kK6xEgPVlG_1jhW_oWz8Ti_MtzU9Wp7utT5LY340em70fJ7n7ffrwGts9Ay50srY6PnyavT8TEyV1uv8sATjToWhHhn__e7pKCg8MQsPMVLcxZYy0h7F3xCERfjjfQ1XpEbPuh-Fc5u1aKSwo-yFtSMIPzotgjNBYVCd0b7NU3iexEylv9T2BxB_TOr_9X9N-k8AAAD__3FHmgU) to the ban. Opponents argue it could prevent [lawful residents](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkD2SgzAMhU8DnRnJ_JgULrZJl223ZGSsgGaNyYATh9vvkCtsLb33zfd-bsONJEicvmlhm973dYic98Ap8TYEin6nwI7iuD5j2o7luctIedCgWwWtQhg8STiGpvTWgb9gyRZN0_Wm6S9YzhbQeVd3aC6u5rZ2DbP5XMn01NVYij3LoEVABNR95ch7HsFgDdh5MEUD7CWtW5VmTvym_R6O9cVbNa5LGeyc0mMv6q9CXwt9zTlXp0Jm_j0fCn39ZJSjqMZZIin3PCRO6rRT93VjmaLSYAB6XW7WP7KEIHt3gqeFJHw46X_rvKz-CwAA___DhXmI) who pose no national security risk from buying property.. The bill now returns to the Senate to consider changes made in the House approval process. If passed, it would go into effect Sept. 1 and only apply to transactions after that date.
## TxDOT Alters Plan to Preserve Historic Ranch
The owner of a 200-year-old Rio Grande ranch has won a legal battle that [prevented the expansion](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkLHOozAQhJ_GdEbrDRgoXFxD9197JdrYTrBkbGRvQnj7E3mFvx2NPn0z_36WHwoxpOdf2rzhzyMvyR81emZflkjJVYr-TsnmV-Jybq8aLB0LAvYSeqlgcRTiuXSNM3dwk2q8UUOnx6EbJ9WsBqm_DahI0YNc50BrrbBDqy2AHvupCeaCQa9AKVA4tndyzlsY1A2UdjCIDrwLnEvLq2f_ofqIZ3770tq8NdGszHsVtz8CZ4HzcRztdlZKlDinkK-SwPlaJXCu-cWr_EIEzlQ42OgFzt9E-ii3EOlZsiyU7Cr54zJLBJxGGLHd170pxu1HiDFUfZk9NwrxK8K_u-9t8H8AAAD__wz3hSc) of Hwy. 83 through his property. After receiving bipartisan support, rancher Rene Ramirez successfully lobbied for TxDOT to reroute the expansion east of the ranch, avoiding the property. Legislators have seized on the fight over the historic ranch to introduce bills that would change the way TxDOT interacts with landowners impacted by proposed new roads or expansions, requiring greater transparency and direct contact with the owners.
## Frisco Hosts ACM Awards
Frisco, Tex. played host to the Academy of Country Music (ACM) Awards on Thursday night for the [60th edition](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkL2uozAUhJ8GOpB_wHYKF9vQZdst0cHnJLFkMLJNHN5-Re4j3HY-afTN_LvPd_DBb8-_sJItn0ecN6o5UCmU5gAbZgi0wObisZV0rkf2DuosmBg7NnaczQg-nPPQol0Y3nhLlutBGT2YG29fljljlDLI1bhoaZQkkFLTgzstDKFsvb3K2MgZ54wL0y-ASI5pLhlXyHQzMEJfYurLiwp9ID_CGd-UehfXNthXKXtu5J9GTI2Yaq19iQjnRRsx7XF3RyhHokZMUCFhbsQU_Ju6JcTnFbq1-wHdd9aXHTtCodwlt4Fgo7ipNlncqw_BZ3U5PVfw4atQfnfc24r_AQAA___C4IJ2) of the event. The biggest winners of the night were Ella Langley, who won a total of eight awards, and Lainey Wilson, who took home the coveted [Entertainer of the Year](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkL2ypCAQhZ9GMyzAHzQg2MRsNt3QaqEd2W3RAhzGt99y6iY3vmFX9-n6vvPnMT3AkfPP37ChTu9lnzzmSJgShonA2wiEM3iznz6FazujM5AnyWXLeMsEnyw4uqamtHrmdhAlaqGarldNP4hy1Utb1-0sBW9xrpUdZrkYrvoOpRzEoKB0-n7GW8GF4EL21QzWouFK1Fx0lqui4Whd2kOVVkz4hrjQtb8wVGbfStJrSkcs6l-FHAs55pwrP5vb4t4Xcjz2g5mT0hnw-8Tuo0KOBM7jxbKjuHsWTsLIPoJgwOJ2sS959rFnkCHYyIDAs79g_t2h9Q4aD5K3quvLoO2RHZGL3Y3_3MDRhzb9rOOXlv8DAAD__xMVk4Q) prize. The event, hosted by Reba McEntire, included musical performances by Clint Black, Dan + Shay, LeAnn Rimes, Rascal Flatts, and the Backstreet Boys, among others. The bill, known as Senate Bill 17, would also give authority to Gov. Greg Abbott to to the ban. Opponents argue it could prevent who pose no national security risk from buying property.. The bill now returns to the Senate to consider changes made in the House approval process. If passed, it would go into effect Sept. 1 and only apply to transactions after that date. TxDOT Alters Plan to Preserve Historic Ranch The owner of a 200-year-old Rio Grande ranch has won a legal battle that of Hwy. 83 through his property. After receiving bipartisan support, rancher Rene Ramirez successfully lobbied for TxDOT to reroute the expansion east of the ranch, avoiding the property. Legislators have seized on the fight over the historic ranch to introduce bills that would change the way TxDOT interacts with landowners impacted by proposed new roads or expansions, requiring greater transparency and direct contact with the owners. name: KamalaHarris.com
subject: Why Democrats are going into Republican districts
body:
All across the country, Democratic elected officials are traveling to Republican states and congressional districts to hold town halls.
# [](https://act.kamalaharris.com/go/2780?t=1&akid=572%2E2563979%2E0uw0zl)

All across the country, Democratic elected officials are traveling to Republican states and congressional districts to hold town halls.
They are doing it to talk about the Trump-Musk reconciliation bill that would take funding from lifesaving programs and give that cash to some of the wealthiest people and corporations in the country.
And they are doing it because Republicans have been told to avoid town halls and meetings in their own districts.
The events have been a huge success. Thousands of people are showing up in places Democrats don't always campaign.
And if we are able to stop this terrible bill, these events will be a big reason why.
Now - what many people don't know is that many of these events are sponsored and organized by the Democratic National Committee. It wouldn't be happening without them.
But it can't happen without a fully-funded DNC. And as we get closer and closer to a vote, we can't afford to stop now. In fact, we need to ratchet UP the pressure.
If everyone reading this donated $20 to the DNC, they would have the resources to hold endless events in Republican districts. But not everyone does give - so Democrats everywhere are counting on people like you who understand the importance of these meetings to chip-in.
[**If stopping the Trump-Musk reconciliation bill feels worth a $20 donation, that is what Kamala Harris and Democrats everywhere are asking you to donate to the DNC today. Help them continue organizing these meetings across the country.**](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em-dnc?refcode=em250510-a&refcode2=572_2563979_0uw0zl&akid=572%2E2563979%2E0uw0zl)

name: Douglas Carswell
subject: Immigration Control – What Should America Do?
body:
| | | [View this email in your browser](https://mailchi.mp/mspolicy/immigrationcontrol?e=5703353baa)
---
| |
---
| _**If you're not in front of our Saturday email, you're behind.**_
---
| Dear Friend, Given the importance of immigration, I thought I should share an article I just wrote for the _Washington Examiner_ on the reforms America needs.
---
|
---
| In his first 100 days, President Donald Trump has made significant strides tackling the problem of illegal immigration. Little has yet been done, however, to overhaul America’s dysfunctional system of legal immigration.

The United States is the preferred destination for would-be migrants from every continent and culture. Yet, given how many people want to come here, America does a remarkably bad job selecting the brightest and the best.

The U.S. grants green cards to roughly 1 million people annually, but surprisingly few are given on the basis of merit or because the recipient possesses specialized skills.

More than half of the green cards are awarded through the family-based immigration system. This tends to favor the relatives of recently naturalized US citizens from Mexico, India, and China. While family ties matter, this means from the outset that over half of green cards go to those with the right relatives, rather than the right skills.

In a typical year, only about a fifth of green cards go to employment-based applicants. While these employment-based programs are supposed to attract skilled workers, in reality those approved are selected more by chance than merit.

Most temporary Employment-based visas are H-1B visas (85,000 a year). Intended for specialty occupations, the H-1B scheme has in the past been gamed by outsourcing firms, which flooded what is essentially a lottery system with entries—often for fairly low-skill tech jobs—securing cheap labor, primarily from India and China. A backlog of H-1B holders awaiting permanent status further clogs the pipeline.

Only those admitted via the O1 (extraordinary ability) and L1 (intracompany transfer) schemes are selected more by merit than chance. Fewer than 5 percent of those granted permanent residence came to America this way.

Then there’s the Diversity Visa program, which is also a lottery, and admits around 50,000 people yearly from “underrepresented” nations like Egypt, Uzbekistan and Pakistan through random selection. Since 1995, 1.5 million have entered this way, with minimal vetting.

Nor is there much merit based selection when it comes to schemes such as Jo Biden’s infamous CHNV Parole initiative, which in 2023 allowed in 30,00 people a month from places like Haiti and Venezuela.

America’s immigration system is flawed, not just because it’s a hodge podge of programs, but because it has been built on a deeply flawed philosophy.

America’s elites have created an immigration system in the belief that human nature is universal. People from every background, they want to believe, are interchangeable. Its thanks to this fairy tale that the United States literally selects at random individuals from places like Mogadishu and settles them in Michigan, all the time expecting they will be like every other Midwesterner. That’s not the way it’s working out in Dearborn or _Hamtramck._

America has long been a melting pot, but as Europe is learning in the wake of large scale Muslim immigration, some parts of the melting pot will not melt unless there’s a degree of cultural compatibility.

**Cultural compatibility matters, and America needs an immigration system that selects for it, as well as for skills.**

Immigrants, as we all know, can be highly entrepreneurial. That’s a reason to be more, rather than less, selective. While over half of all US unicorn companies worth over a billion dollars were founded by immigrants, according to a National Foundation for American Policy study, these enterprising immigrants came from a select number of countries, including India, Israel, Britain and Europe.

America needs to replace the H-1B visa with a greatly expanded, if less onerous, O1 type scheme, prioritizing individuals with exceptional talent. The aim should be to offer entry to real quality applicants, not use the immigration system to hire half price software engineers.

At the same time, America should eliminate the Diversity Immigrant Visa Program entirely. So what if some countries have more to offer America and are therefore overrepresented against others? Not all cultures are equal. Nor are they all equally compatible with the Western way of life.

America should not shy away from capping visas from Muslim-majority countries given the challenges of cultural integration. America should proactively revoke the status of anyone supporting extremist groups such as Hamas, Hezbollah, the Muslim Brotherhood, or Hizb ut-Tahrir, or who holds views about apostacy that are incompatible with the First Amendment.

As an immigrant to America, I found that my daughter did not know the Pledge of Allegiance on her first day at school. We made sure she knew the words as well anyone in her class on her second day. ** I want every immigrant to feel as fortunate as I know I am to be here.**

Have a wonderful weekend!
---
| [Forward this email to a friend!](https://mspolicy.us18.list-manage.com/track/click?u=60d4052c66b6745831ccdf7c8&id=1ba6c9cc40&e=5703353baa "Forward this email to a friend!")
---
| |
---
Warm regards,

Douglas Carswell
President & CEO
---
|
---
| | _ _
You are receiving this email because you opted in via our website.

**Our mailing address is:**
Mississippi Center for Public Policy520 George StJackson, MS 39202-3013
[Add us to your address book](https://mspolicy.us18.list-manage.com/vcard?u=60d4052c66b6745831ccdf7c8&id=2f68021ebd)

Want to change how you receive these emails?
You can [update your preferences](https://mcpp-c58f.mailchimpsites.com/manage/preferences?u=60d4052c66b6745831ccdf7c8&id=2f68021ebd&e=5703353baa&c=8205b04071) or [unsubscribe from this list](https://mspolicy.us18.list-manage.com/unsubscribe?u=60d4052c66b6745831ccdf7c8&id=2f68021ebd&t=b&e=5703353baa&c=8205b04071).

name: Jason Miyares
subject: My American Dream starts with family
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Miyares for Virginia |
body:
Friend, this is why Mother's Day is so special to me.| | | Friend, This Mother’s Day Weekend, I’m reflecting on the two women who have shaped my life in the most profound way—** _my mother_** _and_** _my wife._**
**My mother fled communist Cuba,** chasing the promise of freedom and opportunity to build a better life.
I got to live the **American Dream** because of _her bravery._
---
[](http://links.jasonforvirginia.com/s/c/JtR8u491clSDp3-XKNxTQYkmzJjUiYM5R1SqpHkcV47eobY4y6QFrRYbj-OVCHPRPNfSPcmNE7sqkyDtejGaC7OZdEBcNm_u2PfTT7ny_vW9qvjPkKRhjf1jwALkHk0GgTDO0Ny0eA5Ll1ZZ2oO4VM0_D4o2kRWZGzFnPiixvfGLT3UGkYJRIDfdlpRFkRakHI1GP9hhGFhYuQi5iQ-QcHrr1PF0rgT0Q-VUmfnzLN9Q6Ao7NXsx2T6SQY9_jefaig/jmOoEmxygqxiMPKHVTLUVWPmpXdHlFjb/8)
---
Today, I’m blessed to raise three teenage daughters with **my amazing wife, Page.** _Her love, strength, and faith keep our family grounded—and remind me every day what we’re fighting for._
From my mom’s courage to my wife’s grace, **my life has been shaped by strong women.** And they inspire me to protect the American Dream for _every family in Virginia._
This weekend, as we celebrate mothers, I hope you’ll join me in honoring the women who make freedom worth fighting for.
**Let’s take a moment to thank the moms, grandmothers, and mother figures who shape our families—and our future.**
---
[](http://links.jasonforvirginia.com/s/c/lZs5KLrkFDfmpFZz2beAQahLkZoW9WTuqhgx7GM-wdmshMxmbsJV9T4GfOeAQWbyGSrPiqj01cuyT4h-Q_hxekJmEarJe0wb9m2ItzSI_AEBNBkLKLBpx8i4g7jZ6nIGuJ74xh4gsm9rHY_IOW8ZKZwQNyl7dbhP_OCyoKeAcxa_nGHt5tl690X02duuVYt9rEoHHyp7NlmxahYQct9DbttuqNbDCclTDHPZqdKqarPMlg93YhDl-9HbS2oMUXXNEA/xs15T7ePRhuc9Pbq-UGGti7Rc6ynZZyA/8)
---
Wishing you and your loved ones a joyful Mother’s Day Weekend!
---
— Jason
---
| | |
---
| [](http://links.jasonforvirginia.com/s/c/GaRxOH5gh7V0XIJXyiFVGYlU92Dyy6eKmtfOPBz5HP6eX1oLR6BFabNyIKe9YsbeGlWJHIhbtUB8PQX4gUhFsjFgLlnFIuk9x_MqaPrdxfPMSN3eZOxgM57g_7q3UXQX6WAQ5ykwjAWNDbA7LP3f8t-ME5a8GL8Fl1rwbsuwjzO95mNWfXlMQs5o95Yw6818241SaIHKdgDnfyXjspPHawlrUMaBeGCWuU4IgRya87PHpnWSnWuDsSIrV5lRb2LbJrQyYloNMy7yDCoKuoGp-g9nTkiJXFPlsbKr/Cgf7v-WkIp62u61lmC3RhGAc0o3um4Xt/8)| [](http://links.jasonforvirginia.com/s/c/ssqPGwGF_joMAEffZq4qwLdK1KIU7U4o0WM9xTsLNJZGRnHwuTViENhgEMezujlejpOv6Pl_zWZtN4fnhwYhOb5l_rsSPF3r_3lxM6QsZ1w8D1BgPSnHKCCyjJV3e2Ajp_yEKqNqKvicw5445nzNoVqlcz0dtP-hGlO9hJN6Eg2l8kOUoDff0bL2uh15_3urUifwBj1w8eVDx1OkYONljsK_WgPaIOjz135H1ZRB0-iszNefthukX9-DiAeF2WhRYoA9e56sffoOeaHh3w/MKKkJ84ZMWPSEDbVmV1nj-sKBtRcUJEb/8)| [](http://links.jasonforvirginia.com/s/c/kNW4FW7bSrK9jXXzqQQZekhmby8aHVZADqk3cihyTC_KK6KAR4IrEbWyO7keGdflvm-Aya5rp0_StYbAQRcBnOGxthJngvoP9WYTOgipUTK7nMv65TaHN7XWv0v9KV1eQt9gRZSkrcs84RTTmWh2NMGNQCbq8pThHKMgDL70MryZKo-GxaIrKP4GGV0cMg5jFCrVL0BUAiFAtn5jvKo2LDT787uG4zKHNGsjq3yV83QSdvOsqkNhr0YwbpamQ1WtIm1qzDrUOFv9rd4M3lg/lLiPN-IUKH_tx9Jqf5XtFffnzKgHa8BF/8)

name: Rudy Giuliani
subject: President Trump: “SAVE RUDY!!!”
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Rudy Giuliani Freedom Fund Legal Defense Trust
body:
Democrats think they’ve found a bulletproof loophole...
| [](http://links.freedomtrainalert.com/u/click?_t=2d4f229f156142619b4e4f956d2d65c9&_m=bc90f5c8fd25405ebf2713364f66a49c&_e=Rit1Ts57KeEirhELuhQqrDXjrAePy6W3I-fMNhXzpoMikmRoL8Y-mmL7Va2GQ2gixGF60hxtR8XEOdUFSj7nNTYjdAxNH6_zpIamZF26wIzznqLcJtMd4bpD5z3wQ72NR3d00I-_2DsZz_WBgIMweZ_UElxCWrWLcscd-9h1euTiwpvNOttK5IL8ykYJKKrnc20ZCUjNegR72l9KferX45Z-BdEDIriRzQEMohOW2gfObVaAFOf7PfsGQR_j9JifnRsTFbP60w7bOoBaHPLHi-asuAyoyu5vRQWP8m2IYrNTAcJlrEgQhMDDCgd60S9OdoUF1L35Qfe3iWQ0U2w7m7DshgAUHRrhLU7-zbzu2wNe2QXPfPKpWOven7RYHj98)
---
Fellow Patriot,
---
For the sake of my life, I hope to God they’re wrong...
---
...But Democrats believe they’ve finally found the bulletproof loophole to send me to prison until I’m 100 years old.
---
Because the Constitution doesn’t give the President the power to issue pardons for _state-level_ crimes, a corrupt Democrat Attorney General is forcing me to stand trial in _state_ court so that President Trump cannot save me from this grave injustice.
---
But of course President Trump isn’t just going to roll over to the Left. Since _he_ knows he cannot save me with a pardon, he’s calling on _YOU_ to join the cause to save me.

clean_body: This is your final opportunity, Fellow Patriot! The emergency card is closing soon. | | | | | | Friend, Let's remind the world that courage still counts. | In moments of bold leadership and courage, we must not stay silent--we must show our gratitude. That's why I'm reaching out one last time. Our emergency card is about to close, and you still haven't signed your name, Friend! Elon has stood strong for free speech, innovation, and against the radical waste in Washington --and now, it 's our turn to stand up and say thank you. "This is your final opportunity to join us, Friend!" | | | | | President Trump made history with his return to the White House & it's time for our country to rally behind the values that make us Great- AMERICA IS BACK! You can flaunt your support for a stronger America by contributing a one-time gift of $10 to claim your free bumper sticker. | | "CLAIM YOUR STICKER" "These stickers are an extremely limited one-time print, so be sure to secure yours while they remain in stock."** | PAID FOR BY TIM SCOTT FOR AMERICA Tim Scott for America 7620 Rivers Ave, Ste. 370 #312 North Charleston, South Carolina 294067 | To ensure that you don't miss out on important messages, please add "info@email.votetimscott.com" to your address book. name: Ruben Gallego
subject: Let me explain why I am in Pennsylvania today
disclaimer_text: Paid For By Gallego for Arizona
body:
Good morning from Bucks County, Pennsylvania.
#

Good morning from Bucks County, Pennsylvania.
In a moment, I am going to ask you to make a donation to help me continue this work on the road, but first you deserve to know why I am here – on the other side of the country, Arizona.
Here's the truth:
We have a president hell-bent on pushing aggressive tariff policies that are raising prices on American households, and Elon Musk is cutting programs our kids and seniors rely on.
All the while, the conversations happening in Washington, D.C. are disconnected from the conversations happening at kitchen tables.
Yet, despite all this, Democrats still look like the party that brought a knife to a gunfight.
As a Marine, standing up for what’s right is second nature to me. I understand the pressure families in Pennsylvania are under right now because I’ve lived it. I grew up working any job I could — a meatpacking plant, a hot dog stand, cleaning dorm rooms in college — just to make ends meet.
That’s why I’m coming to Pennsylvania. The Keystone State, like Arizona, is a battleground where the voices of hardworking families must be heard and their concerns taken seriously.
I can’t stand idle, so I’m heading to Bucks County to turn up the heat and show Pennsylvanians what’s at stake — their healthcare, retirement security, and children’s future.
The stakes are too high for anyone to stay on the sidelines. I’m going to make sure the voices of real people, hardworking Americans, are heard where it counts.
That's why I am here — and that is work I am going to continue to do. I'd appreciate your support.
[**Please make a contribution of $5, $50 or whatever you can afford to help me continue to turn up the heat and show all Americans what is at stake right now. I can't do it without you.**](https://act.gallegoforarizona.com/go/1000?c0=ruben-em-may25.XT1Xme&refcode=em250510-1-90&t=1&akid=3862%2E734936%2EeWA6As)
_If you 've stored your info with ActBlue, we'll process your contribution instantly._

clean_body: Good morning from Bucks County, Pennsylvania. Good morning from Bucks County, Pennsylvania. In a moment, I am going to ask you to make a donation to help me continue this work on the road, but first you deserve to know why I am here – on the other side of the country, Arizona. Here's the truth: We have a president hell-bent on pushing aggressive tariff policies that are raising prices on American households, and Elon Musk is cutting programs our kids and seniors rely on. All the while, the conversations happening in Washington, D.C. are disconnected from the conversations happening at kitchen tables. Yet, despite all this, Democrats still look like the party that brought a knife to a gunfight. As a Marine, standing up for what’s right is second nature to me. I understand the pressure families in Pennsylvania are under right now because I’ve lived it. I grew up working any job I could — a meatpacking plant, a hot dog stand, cleaning dorm rooms in college — just to make ends meet. That’s why I’m coming to Pennsylvania. The Keystone State, like Arizona, is a battleground where the voices of hardworking families must be heard and their concerns taken seriously. I can’t stand idle, so I’m heading to Bucks County to turn up the heat and show Pennsylvanians what’s at stake — their healthcare, retirement security, and children’s future. The stakes are too high for anyone to stay on the sidelines. I’m going to make sure the voices of real people, hardworking Americans, are heard where it counts. That's why I am here — and that is work I am going to continue to do. I'd appreciate your support. If you 've stored your info with ActBlue, we'll process your contribution instantly. | | ** | | | | | | | ** Semper Fi, Ruben Gallego Paid For By Gallego for Arizona Use of military rank, job titles, and photographs in uniform do not imply endorsement by the Department of the Navy or the Department of Defense.

Colonel Stevenson serves as House Minority Floor Leader in the Kentucky Legislature. She is often the only Democratic member to break through to Republican leadership to make the bad bills better. Her Republican opponent in the General election will likely be a minion of Mitch McConnell – **the longest-serving Republican party leader in U.S. Senate history.**

McConnell developed a reputation as “Dr. No” during former President Obama’s term. He blocked Obama’s pick for a vacancy on the Supreme Court, paving the way for Roe v. Wade to be overturned. He unified Republicans against the Affordable Care Act, and then, after it passed, he sought to undo the law. He has voted against IVF. He has an A+ rating with the Susan B. Anthony Pro-Life America scorecard for consistently voting against reproductive rights. He voted against the Family and Medical Leave Act, opposes same-sex marriage, voted against the Equal Pay Act, voted against raising the federal minimum wage... and so much more.

Mitch McConnell has been horrible for America, and after enduring him for decades, the people of Kentucky – and our country as a whole – deserve better. **This is truly a once-in-a-generation chance to elect someone amazing to replace him as his seat in Washington is FINALLY open.**

**That is where Col. Stevenson comes in.**

[**From negotiating hostage crises, prosecuting child sex criminals, violent crimes, and more, Colonel Stevenson has served this country with honor. Now, she needs our help to take her experience in public service to Washington. Help ensure Col. Stevenson’s campaign has the funding it will need to be successful by pitching in a split $20.25 or more contribution today!**](https://click.everyaction.com/k/107439402/544698028/-527673297?refcode=EM_F_250510stevenson_N_CB_NO_8_G_N_L2&amount=20.25&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAwNy8xLzg4NDk1IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjQ4ODU5NWRmLWEyMmQtZjAxMS04YjNkLTYwNDViZGVkOGNjYSIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=QNQDxJwIhKDZD1YvoS-dEz2xymKhrD4JHzgxoJTuHOY=)

Together,

Charlotte Bell
Policy Researcher
Her Bold Move
| | Her Bold Move is the fastest-growing support network for women running for political office in America. As dangerous laws about women get passed in states with the lowest numbers of women in government (11%, 12%....), we're BREAKING the male supermajorities across those states.

With Thursday, May 15 quickly approaching, you won't want to miss out on the Texas World Premiere of [Follow the Silenced](https://xdzgmz.clicks.mlsend.com/tj/cl/eyJ2Ijoie1wiYVwiOjEyMDEyNjAsXCJsXCI6MTU0MDE4ODM0NjEyNzUwMDc0LFwiclwiOjE1NDAxODg3MzQxNTMwNDU3NH0iLCJzIjoiZmE5YWQxNjRjZTdlMjc2ZCJ9), which follows individuals who were brave enough to speak out after they were injured and ignored by Big Pharma's Covid-19 vaccine clinical trials. Texans for Vaccine Choice is excited to have partnered with the filmmakers in this documentary's production, and we are looking forward to an amazing premiere in Austin, where we'll be joined by some big names like: **🌟Matthew Guthrie & Tyler Lindsay**, Filmmakers

**🌟Dr. Mary Talley Bowden** , Physician and medical liberty advocate
**🌟Dr. Judy Mikovits** , Molecular Biologist and COVID whistleblower
**🌟Brianne Dressen** , React19 founder

name: Kristen McDonald Rivet
subject: I was floored when I got this news.
body:
|
#
This is stunning, folks. Earlier this week, news broke that a GOP-linked dark money group, the American Action Network, was gearing up to attack me. **We just got more details: it's a massive $300,000+ ad campaign right here in Michigan’s 8th District. As you read this, we’re being attacked on cable and broadcast TV. It's a full-blown misinformation blitz -- a full year and a half before the election. ** To call this aggressive would be an understatement. There’s no declared Republican in our race, but that’s not stopping Elon Musk and the Republicans' dark money machine from going on the attack early in what we already knew would be a brutal campaign to win our Trump+2 district. **And here’s a reality check: our fundraising is not keeping pace.** The fact is with so much going on in Washington, we've had much less time to fundraise for my reelection, and we need to get moving to compete with this dark money blitz. That’s why I’m reaching out today: [**We set an emergency goal of raising $10,000 before midnight tonight to respond and send a message that MI-08 is not for sale. Can you rush in $3 today? Please know that I wouldn't be asking if it wasn’t important.**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/107441791/544702527/846650059?refcode=em-5.9.25-all-a&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzMvMS85NjAyNCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI2OGI0MGNmMi1hNDJkLWYwMTEtOGIzZC02MDQ1YmRlZDhjY2EiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=utv_yl32z0G8T-tNjeETVQWnvotcmsHGJiRzPHlY2xQ=) __If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: __ | | | **[Chip in $10 Now](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/107441792/544702528/846650059?refcode=em-5.9.25-all-a&express_lane=true&amount=10&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzMvMS85NjAyNCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI2OGI0MGNmMi1hNDJkLWYwMTEtOGIzZC02MDQ1YmRlZDhjY2EiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=utv_yl32z0G8T-tNjeETVQWnvotcmsHGJiRzPHlY2xQ=)**

name: Sarah McBride
subject: This has felt like a hell of a decade
disclaimer_text: Paid for by McBride for Delaware
body:
My job is to speak out, and I intend to do just that.
#
I’ve been in Congress for just over 100 days, and it’s felt like a hell of a decade.
I’m sure I don’t need to tell you that what the Trump administration is doing is _unprecedented._ From attacks on public services to deliberate efforts to chip away at the foundation of our democracy, the stakes are high — and deeply personal for the people I represent.
But despite all the chaos and attacks, I’m committed more than ever to defending Delaware, pushing back against this administration, and finding opportunities to make progress — even when it’s hard. _Especially when it’s hard._
I refuse to give in to the culture of fear that the Trump administration is creating across this country. **My job is to speak out, and I intend to do just that.**
[**If you’re in the position to do so, will you donate $10, $20, or even $50 to help me continue to push back against this administration's attacks on all of us?**](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/mcb-em-20250510-1?refcode=em20250510-1979&refcode2=1979_21716_Uwi8tF&akid=1979%2E21716%2EUwi8tF)
**[Donate »](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/mcb-em-20250510-1?refcode=em20250510-1979&refcode2=1979_21716_Uwi8tF&akid=1979%2E21716%2EUwi8tF)**
Thank you for standing with me,
Sarah
* * *
**[Donate »](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/mcb-em-footer?refcode=em20250510-1&t=1&refcode2=1979_21716_Uwi8tF&akid=1979%2E21716%2EUwi8tF)**
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is a critical way we communicate with grassroots supporters like you. However, if you would like to change your subscription preferences, here are some options:
* [Receive Fewer Emails](https://act.sarahmcbride.com/signup/receive_fewer_emails/?t=2&akid=1979%2E21716%2EUwi8tF)
* [Update Your Information](https://act.sarahmcbride.com/signup/update_your_information/?t=3&akid=1979%2E21716%2EUwi8tF)
* [Unsubscribe](https://act.sarahmcbride.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=4&akid=1979%2E21716%2EUwi8tF)

Email us: [info@sarahmcbride.com](mailto:info@sarahmcbride.com)

[**Check out the op-ed here: Kadida Kenner: State government can secure our access to the ballot →**](https://u44255144.ct.sendgrid.net/ls/click?upn=u001.ekJ0nzuGxxKMd6ADw0n9YoM5mfOOfnlKU6jMiEEEJx4Dye9OQigKmyTfa2Kf4U7l3rR-2BQGEVadC35ayXl1RC1E0Xe7CvJ53MldZWae-2Fv9qOPorqWPdSIEhSnLeFDKoTwDC3QBsFYr4kDSG-2FNj-2BlIsyFBdB2gJiDevPadAP0gEAc-3DXPLC_9XnTPLnaEjN9OHWhKYUffDeECIVzR9M-2B5LaO5RH8NVAKmTIthZAqug-2BBe3TY9jweA5gYJTWBMeK2mbEu1GsUAWbMxqbbanoccXqIiueqpc6DIzHn4ElffI7A3Y14vKdCNksTNdqpcPsZ-2Fywu-2FMuXA4y3CGiU9X4tiAddJTMlizHBV3fCrFnIJPehcpV1HfrYCvSXSYBtXj0TOrTSqMrO1uE-2B0mamXDOURNjQw-2F0-2FzdoazQ5TIccG0MSPhCd-2FuS4-2FEybn-2B5HqyucpBkR4Y18Ux-2BaGHfoqZYp8q57cHcELe6S23m4JYhm48WUGsQIugvfZHskKlHmtz2IulrXcIWS3gHRq3WmjvIVcaF8usbL5M7UDCLyv5aC-2F8rSGQh0nvjGl)
Here is a snippet of the article:

_“What we see consistently on the ground in our communities is that the vast majority of everyday voters have lots of other things they’re focused on other than politics. They’re worried about things like putting food on the table, keeping an affordable roof over their family’s heads, getting the kids to after school activities on time and taking care of aging relatives. The average Pennsylvanian simply doesn’t have the time to tune into elections until the very end."_

_“If we allowed eligible citizens to register to vote at any point, up to and including Election Day, we could bring more of communities’ voices to the table and increase voter participation. Democracy only truly functions for all people when every single voter is heard and every single ballot is counted.” _

**As Kadida stated, it is time that our elected officials put the will of the people first and work to ensure that everyone has the ability to participate in the democratic process.**[**You can continue reading Kadida’s op ed here.**](https://u44255144.ct.sendgrid.net/ls/click?upn=u001.ekJ0nzuGxxKMd6ADw0n9YoM5mfOOfnlKU6jMiEEEJx4Dye9OQigKmyTfa2Kf4U7l3rR-2BQGEVadC35ayXl1RC1E0Xe7CvJ53MldZWae-2Fv9qOPorqWPdSIEhSnLeFDKoTwDC3QBsFYr4kDSG-2FNj-2BlIsyFBdB2gJiDevPadAP0gEAc-3DVyTT_9XnTPLnaEjN9OHWhKYUffDeECIVzR9M-2B5LaO5RH8NVAKmTIthZAqug-2BBe3TY9jweA5gYJTWBMeK2mbEu1GsUAWbMxqbbanoccXqIiueqpc6DIzHn4ElffI7A3Y14vKdCNksTNdqpcPsZ-2Fywu-2FMuXA4y3CGiU9X4tiAddJTMlizHBV3fCrFnIJPehcpV1HfrYCvSXSYBtXj0TOrTSqMrO1uE-2B0mamXDOURNjQw-2F0-2FzdoargFUmkDP3TE7tfghYk2TgeFwjmsrdH9W8hjEFM0Jp9rmlVQc4hKQyJ3SDlgkDaKfTqIvZm6V9-2BWJkvSbCHcyFau1m0znG94qgSe17h4JFO-2FfD7Uhq1BCAEttm6OQBKG1HA6CrJpTr9I8-2BM7xxPIm)

_Donations to the New Pennsylvania Project supports our staff’s community engagement efforts including; organizing and hosting town halls, educational webinars, community conversations, and creating digital content, bi-weekly blasts and social media to reach the communities we engage year-round._
---
| | _The New Pennsylvania Project (NPP) is a voting rights organization with a year-round primary focus on voter registration, civic education, and mobilization. NPP centers historically disenfranchised and often neglected Black, Indigenous and other people of color, immigrant communities and the youth in our work. Through civic engagement, we ensure all eligible voters feel compelled to exercise their freedom to vote in the Commonwealth._

clean_body: | | | Fellow activist, Recently, our CEO, Kadida Kenner, published an article with Trib Total Media , and we wanted to make sure you saw it. Here is a snippet of the article: “What we see consistently on the ground in our communities is that the vast majority of everyday voters have lots of other things they’re focused on other than politics. They’re worried about things like putting food on the table, keeping an affordable roof over their family’s heads, getting the kids to after school activities on time and taking care of aging relatives. The average Pennsylvanian simply doesn’t have the time to tune into elections until the very end." “If we allowed eligible citizens to register to vote at any point, up to and including Election Day, we could bring more of communities’ voices to the table and increase voter participation. Democracy only truly functions for all people when every single voter is heard and every single ballot is counted.” As Kadida stated, it is time that our elected officials put the will of the people first and work to ensure that everyone has the ability to participate in the democratic process. Together, Team NPP P.S. Our team is working hard to ensure that voters have what they need to make their voices heard in every election, two times a year. Donations to the New Pennsylvania Project supports our staff’s community engagement efforts including; organizing and hosting town halls, educational webinars, community conversations, and creating digital content, bi-weekly blasts and social media to reach the communities we engage year-round. | | The New Pennsylvania Project (NPP) is a voting rights organization with a year-round primary focus on voter registration, civic education, and mobilization. NPP centers historically disenfranchised and often neglected Black, Indigenous and other people of color, immigrant communities and the youth in our work. Through civic engagement, we ensure all eligible voters feel compelled to exercise their freedom to vote in the Commonwealth. Will you support us with a monthly contribution today? || || || The New Pennsylvania Project is a 501(c)(4). Contributions are not for charitable purposes and are not tax deductible. | Paid for by the New Pennsylvania Project Our Contact Information New Pennsylvania ProjectP.O. Box 443 West Chester, PA 193814126996771 | | | |

name: Carol Obando-Derstine
subject: I’m running for Congress in Pennsylvania’s 7th District!
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Blue Amp Action PAC
body:
| | | |
---
| | | | |
---
| _You're getting this message because you’ve supported good causes and Democratic candidates. We need your support to continue fighting for our democracy. We love having you in our network, but if you no longer wish to hear from us, you can[unsubscribe here](https://default.salsalabs.org/Tef25a136-ac56-45ab-be89-d820b81698cd/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)._
---
| [](https://default.salsalabs.org/T1c889dcf-996f-45df-95ad-de7ed0b3782f/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9) Hi Peter, I’m Carol Obando-Derstine, and I’m excited to announce that I’m running for Congress in Pennsylvania’s 7th District! This race is going to be one of the most competitive in the country in 2026, and I’m determined to flip it from red to blue. But to do it, I need to know that you’re with me. [Will you join us by splitting a donation between Blue Amp Action and my campaign today?](https://default.salsalabs.org/T0bae4f2b-7c67-4791-9c72-1711952abdf7/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)
---
| | [JOIN OUR CAMPAIGN](https://default.salsalabs.org/T1173fe62-0bbc-45ab-97ba-7a4cf9213e66/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)
---
| I’m the daughter of two working-class union members. My family immigrated to the United States when I was just 3 years old. We came with all the hopes and dreams of so many immigrants before us who wanted the best for their families. While there were challenges along the way, my family built the better life that they were looking for in the U.S. As I watched my parents make sacrifices in order to provide for our family, I learned the value of a strong work ethic. That work ethic led me to serve in a variety of roles: youth advocate, nonprofit leader, public servant, educator, and engineer. While I’ve held different titles, my dedication to serving my community has remained unchanged. And I’ve always strived to be worthy of the generosity this country has shown my family and me. Right now, our current representation doesn’t understand the challenges that our community is facing. I won’t stand by as extreme Republicans like Ryan Mackenzie work with Donald Trump and Elon Musk to chaotically implement tariffs that jack up costs for Pennsylvania families while they cut taxes for their billionaire donors. I’m running for Congress because this district deserves better. I will stand up for the district and fight for real economic change, to build an economy where working families like the one I grew up in can afford housing, groceries and utilities, and have a pathway to real prosperity. [I’m building a people-powered campaign, and to do that, I need you with me. Can I count on you to split a donation between Blue Amp Action and my campaign today?](https://default.salsalabs.org/T86d1d00b-edb5-4316-9c2c-1b7d817783ec/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)
---
| | [DONATE $10 >>](https://default.salsalabs.org/Tf34cc802-8194-4a14-a681-c4567a6d5c6f/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)
---
|
---
| | | | [DONATE $25 >>](https://default.salsalabs.org/Tfb2b0b11-ef28-47dc-81b2-bcc2e37e6d74/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)
---

---
| | | Thank you, Carol Obando-Derstine

| | | About Blue Amp Action: We cannot sugarcoat the current situation. Right-wing authoritarianism, defeated by a worldwide coalition during WWII, was only in hibernation. Over the past decade it has infected democratic societies--including ours. It is as simple as it is terrifying: What we do now will define the future of our country and world. We at Blue Amp Action are dedicated to liberal, open societies, where reason and science are applied to solve our problems; where civil rights--including voting rights!--are protected for all. We fight for these values every day. Speaking up on our platforms, raising the voices and the political prospects of liberal pols and pundits. **And savvy grassroots activists like you know, just like we do, that we can’t stop watching the people we help elect:_ after the elections, we have to keep organizing support for the policies we like and pushing back against the ones we don’t._** **__When the public tunes out between elections and the only people our elected officials hear from are the paid lobbyists of wealthy corporations, our government stops being accountable to the needs of ordinary families.__** **Democracy never sleeps, and neither can we.** So we ask that you support our work, so we can continue to engage in this fight every moment of every day. So our kids grow up in democracy, not dystopia. Thank you for your support for all we do! — _You're getting this message because you’ve supported good causes and Democratic candidates. We need your support to continue fighting for our democracy. We love having you in our network, but if you no longer wish to hear from us, you can[unsubscribe here](https://default.salsalabs.org/T92fd2fca-3557-440b-a37e-a53aae915621/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)._

We love having you in our network, but if you no longer wish to hear from us, you can._ | Paid for by Blue Amp Action PAC | | or

name: Byron Donalds
subject: My mother would’ve ripped DEI to shreds
body:
She always taught me our country was built on hard work
| | | | |
---
[](http://links.g4yamerica.com/u/click?_t=cc7dda5c2cc64061900678c88bd3a18a&_m=f5fe74acd986457784da30cf59b612ed&_e=YNGbM5w_q7ubOeFhb0PY55zeNnOyQXV2oggN2afwHmEy8jZOpScrZ-IgZpKMYtthf--U3hcMgqR_KjCDqeLpEh76R0l7ULQE0t_nDivnKRqf6fNuFt_nDYrf7eyY5i55Z7mR8byWIYrVQVDV2vld5_nzQChUxKbyIo0iJwtqxedVMP5jPBbL_Pw8dR2m6BfAVC4j9-d7FXy_sEHdVUCgRWEwgpjQF9yKoCW4loaHEwtF5OymHBD8RZuXSD3gssFF0XZdimqxDye9nXOJW8WsnfpvAlOOoBA7_CZq1vJOg3Ree9Ll1m3SbMxZMn968hjLchCdCNf2oa1r-e3YhVuimRfyOGdrS5Zurd-7_Bw9i9ZkVoGW-bQTiJvZrrsYmdZv1jtCmC4gqPEv58atxA05kDUJm0uI-j35EXXeSHdfMsBFNwmds_sVVeaw5rntSiNx)

I was raised by a single mother.
I learned to fight with my back against the wall with no way out but through.
So when the radical left Democrats get up in my face & call me a “race-traitor” after I applauded Trump for abolishing DEI, I’m left speechless.
My mom _**never**_ taught me to hate my fellow Americans.
She always instilled in me the values of _hard work_ and _merit._
**If I told her I was a _victim_ like someone in the Woke Mob, she’d probably just laugh like it’s some kind of joke.**
**That’s the America I believe in**. And that’s why I’m a MAGA Patriot who loves President Trump and the America First Movement.
But after I stood with President Trump as he abolished DEI, the attacks against me have been horrendous.
The race-obsessed left just sees me as another Black Man who should sit down, shut up, and vote blue for the rest of my life.
Their hypocrisy _disgusts_ me, and I can brush off most of their attacks, but here’s the _**[real problem](http://links.g4yamerica.com/u/click?_t=cc7dda5c2cc64061900678c88bd3a18a&_m=f5fe74acd986457784da30cf59b612ed&_e=YNGbM5w_q7ubOeFhb0PY55zeNnOyQXV2oggN2afwHmEy8jZOpScrZ-IgZpKMYtthf--U3hcMgqR_KjCDqeLpEh76R0l7ULQE0t_nDivnKRqf6fNuFt_nDYrf7eyY5i55Z7mR8byWIYrVQVDV2vld5_nzQChUxKbyIo0iJwtqxedVMP5jPBbL_Pw8dR2m6BfAVC4j9-d7FXy_sEHdVUCgRWEwgpjQF9yKoCW4loaHEwtF5OymHBD8RZuXSD3gssFF0XZdimqxDye9nXOJW8WsnfpvAlOOoBA7_CZq1vJOg3TkfPDH3pZZeeroi1PIoUbQU_TpavWG3tsuKpRFFBlB-ginkti5NW-TSrKXcVjtjjJ07rAGjjdJv2AzUKqDWHseW7YKLVKz1UoF6uwe5FuqHYnO44Ubpzkh7aDAvU2YN6EBudtnY7F_05zRUdpQsRtT)**_ that I can’t solve on my own.
Right now I’m running for Governor in Florida, and truth be told the radical-left is dropping the hammer on me. They’re prepared to spend **millions of dollars** to prop-up a Democrat that could flip MAGA’s hometurf **BLUE**.
I’m committed to being President Trump’s STRONGEST Governor ally in the nation. But I can’t do this alone.
_**[So before the day is over, can I count on your support to keep me in the fight?](http://links.g4yamerica.com/u/click?_t=cc7dda5c2cc64061900678c88bd3a18a&_m=f5fe74acd986457784da30cf59b612ed&_e=YNGbM5w_q7ubOeFhb0PY55zeNnOyQXV2oggN2afwHmEy8jZOpScrZ-IgZpKMYtthf--U3hcMgqR_KjCDqeLpEh76R0l7ULQE0t_nDivnKRqf6fNuFt_nDYrf7eyY5i55Z7mR8byWIYrVQVDV2vld5_nzQChUxKbyIo0iJwtqxedVMP5jPBbL_Pw8dR2m6BfAVC4j9-d7FXy_sEHdVUCgRWEwgpjQF9yKoCW4loaHEwtF5OymHBD8RZuXSD3gssFF0XZdimqxDye9nXOJW8WsnfpvAlOOoBA7_CZq1vJOg3RuQFDjMVa1vlo8wQaWuc1x2zvFxG-PE6PUpNAO-KGVBEwej3t6IlliwIrtfKC30ROG6osq-uKq7SuMIxNcnQAK3-n3d4lIxsGIwC8EQMbUFEiRcy6OSvniFyGe_zv5TbjZjkv7ybZJaOi0QibHgqku)**_

name: Gabby Giffords
subject: Mark is under attack
disclaimer_text: Paid For By Mark Kelly for Senate
body:
Trump’s political advisor, Roger Stone, is calling for Mark to be charged with treason and executed.
#

It’s Gabby Giffords, and Mark is under attack.
First, Mark called out Trump for weakening our national security, so Elon Musk called him a traitor.
Then, Mark called out Trump for his corruption.
And now, Trump’s political advisor, Roger Stone, is calling for Mark to be charged with treason and _executed_.
My family knows what political violence looks like, and Roger Stone calling for my husband’s execution is beyond the pale. He should be ashamed.
But we see this threat for what it is: They’re scared of Mark and want to stop him.
We have to step up and show Mark we have his back in this fight.

Peter, it’s been

Scott Jennings **[sparred with transgender former Naval officer Alaina Kupec](https://substack.com/redirect/c7a66f59-e421-42f5-ac3a-9ed79790b258?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) **on CNN**** about outrage over Trump's plan to make the United States Military less woke and more focussed on military readiness.
**[He said the quiet part out loud...](https://substack.com/redirect/c7a66f59-e421-42f5-ac3a-9ed79790b258?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**
[Watch Video](https://substack.com/redirect/c7a66f59-e421-42f5-ac3a-9ed79790b258?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
[](https://substack.com/redirect/c7a66f59-e421-42f5-ac3a-9ed79790b258?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
During the roundtable debate, several CNN panelist including Kupec **[defended transgender soldiers as martyrs](https://substack.com/redirect/c7a66f59-e421-42f5-ac3a-9ed79790b258?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** , making them out as victims of Trump's transgender military ban.
Host Abbey Phillip asked a bold question, likening Trump's policy to racial discrimination.
"So if he decides who wakes up tomorrow and says, 'I think it's bad for morale. It's bad for cohesiveness, for there to be, black people in the military, Latino people in the military...' What's the difference between that and what we're seeing here?" Phillip asked to panel.
Jennings didn't hesitate to respond, **["This is ridiculous, this is a ridiculous argument!" ](https://substack.com/redirect/c7a66f59-e421-42f5-ac3a-9ed79790b258?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**
"The President and Sec. of Defense, they believe that readiness and unit cohesion and overall operations of the military are impacted by this. That is their opinion. And that is the opinion of the commander in chief, who the Constitution gives broad latitude to run the armed forces," he added.
"That's their opinion, you're aloud to have one," Jennings said nodding towards Kupec, who had earlier challenged Trump's right to bar trans personnel. "You're also aloud to run for president and become commander-in-chief yourself!"
Conservative commentator Shermichael Singleton weighed in on Phillip comparing gender identity and race, "Frankly, **[I personally think there's a difference](https://substack.com/redirect/c7a66f59-e421-42f5-ac3a-9ed79790b258?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** , and I'm just going to leave it at that."
"Are you saying that the quality of race and the quality of transgender are the same?!" Jennings asked Phillip, still flabbergasted by her far-reaching hypothetical.
Watch the chaotic segment go off the rails:
[Watch Video](https://substack.com/redirect/c7a66f59-e421-42f5-ac3a-9ed79790b258?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)

Secretary of Homeland Security Kristi Noem **[went toe-to-toe with one of the wokest Democrats](https://substack.com/redirect/123f269d-f410-4556-9bb5-431b2a9ad688?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** in congress, Rep. Rosa DeLauro.

**["Ma'am, we still have claims open from Hurricane Katrina,"](https://substack.com/redirect/ceba81de-a839-4904-b8dc-a918917f0e15?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** Noem shot back.
